BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Rivera IT Solutions is currently sole-operated on evenings and weekends. This analysis models the impact of a part-time 1099 subcontractor (10 hrs/week) starting Q3 2026 vs. staying solo.

CurrentState.Note: Owner draws $2,500/mo to personal checking; the remainder is retained for taxes and equipment.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Add 1099 subcontractor at $50/hr, 10 hrs/wk (~$2,000/mo)
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Net income drops until additional client hours are billed. Break-even requires 5 additional retainer clients OR one large audit engagement per month.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Stay solo, invest $850/mo into targeted marketing + certifications
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Lower risk; only 2 new small clients needed to offset the $850 increase. Preserves flexibility for Ironclad day job + CISSP prep + coaching Marcus's Little League team.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Scenario B (stay solo + marketing spend) is the better fit while Justin is still preparing for the Dec 5 CISSP exam and the Ironclad security audit on Nov 2. Revisit subcontractor decision in Q1 2027.
ActionItems: Set monthly marketing budget to $150 through 2026-Q4 and measure inbound lead volume.
Revisit hiring a 1099 subcontractor in Q1 2027 after CISSP results and Ironclad audit are complete.
